TABLE.NAME: confcheckin
TABLE.COMMENT: Main table for Conference Check-in activity instances

FIELD.NAME: course
FIELD.COMMENT: Course this activity belongs to

FIELD.NAME: name
FIELD.COMMENT: Activity name

FIELD.NAME: intro
FIELD.COMMENT: Activity description shown on course page

FIELD.NAME: introformat
FIELD.COMMENT: Format of the intro field

FIELD.NAME: confprogramcmid
FIELD.COMMENT: Course-module id of the mod_confprogram instance this checkin instance checks presenter-ticket eligibility against. Nullable (Phase 4.3): unlike mod_confscheduler's confprogramcmid (a hard requirement), a confcheckin instance with no link simply has no usable presenteronly ticket types -- see classes/local/eligibility.php's docblock for the exact behaviour

FIELD.NAME: paymentaccountid
FIELD.COMMENT: core_payment account id (payment.accountid) this instance's paid ticket types are payable to. Nullable: an instance selling only free/promo ticket types never needs one. Not a real foreign key: core_payment's account table lives outside this plugin's install.xml lineage

TABLE.NAME: confcheckin_tickettype
TABLE.COMMENT: Organiser-defined ticket types for a confcheckin instance (e.g. 1-Day Pass, Presenter, Student). price/currency live here, one per ticket type, rather than on a separate payment-config table, since each ticket type can have its own price in a typical multi-tier-ticket conference model. price/currency are TYPE=char to match core_payment's own amount/currency field convention (verified against {payments}.amount/{payments}.currency and enrol_fee's {enrol}.cost/{enrol}.currency in this Moodle checkout: both are stored as a decimal string in a char(20) field plus a char(3) ISO 4217 currency code, not an integer minor-unit amount), so Phase 4.3's core_payment integration needs no schema change here.

FIELD.NAME: confcheckin
FIELD.COMMENT: Foreign key to confcheckin table

FIELD.NAME: name
FIELD.COMMENT: Ticket type name, e.g. 1-Day Pass, Presenter, Student

FIELD.NAME: price
FIELD.COMMENT: Decimal price string, matching core_payment's {payments}.amount / enrol_fee's {enrol}.cost field convention (char(20) decimal string, not integer minor units)

FIELD.NAME: currency
FIELD.COMMENT: ISO 4217 currency code, matching core_payment's {payments}.currency / enrol_fee's {enrol}.currency field convention (char(3))

FIELD.NAME: capacity
FIELD.COMMENT: Maximum tickets of this type; null means unlimited

FIELD.NAME: presenteronly
FIELD.COMMENT: Whether this ticket type is only purchasable/claimable by a user who is an accepted-submission speaker, checked via mod_confprogram's API in a later phase

FIELD.NAME: validfrom
FIELD.COMMENT: Unix timestamp; ticket only admits from this time onward. Null means no restriction

FIELD.NAME: validto
FIELD.COMMENT: Unix timestamp; ticket only admits until this time. Null means no restriction

FIELD.NAME: visible
FIELD.COMMENT: Whether this ticket type is offered for purchase; 0 hides it without deleting it

FIELD.NAME: soldcount
FIELD.COMMENT: Number of confcheckin_ticket rows currently issued for this ticket type (purchase+free+promo+grant combined). Maintained by classes/local/ticket_service.php's atomic reserve-and-increment logic (SELECT ... FOR UPDATE inside a delegated transaction), NOT by counting confcheckin_ticket rows on demand, so that a capacity check-and-increment is a single indivisible operation under concurrent purchases -- see that class's docblock for the full race-condition rationale. Decremented by revoke_ticket() (Phase 4.5 follow-up: manual revocation of an auto-granted ticket from the orphaned-tickets report), which is the only way a ticket is ever removed post-issuance

FIELD.NAME: groupid
FIELD.COMMENT: A course group id (Phase 4.5 follow-up): a user joining this group is automatically issued a free ticket of this type (origin 'grant'), kept in sync via classes/observer.php's group_member_added handler. Null means no group-based auto-grant. Mutually exclusive with enrolid (enforced in classes/form/tickettype_form.php's validation, not a DB constraint). Not a real foreign key: {groups} lives outside this plugin's install.xml lineage

FIELD.NAME: enrolid
FIELD.COMMENT: A course enrolment method instance id, i.e. {enrol}.id (Phase 4.5 follow-up): a user enrolled via this specific method instance is automatically issued a free ticket of this type (origin 'grant'), kept in sync via classes/observer.php's user_enrolment_created handler. Null means no enrolment-based auto-grant. Mutually exclusive with groupid. Not a real foreign key: {enrol} lives outside this plugin's install.xml lineage

TABLE.NAME: confcheckin_ticket
TABLE.COMMENT: An issued ticket: one row per attendee per confcheckin instance. At most one active ticket per user per instance is a reasonable business rule but is deliberately NOT enforced via a DB unique constraint here, since a later phase may need to allow re-purchase after a refund/cancellation. This table does NOT itself store payment amount/status -- that lives in core_payment's own {payments} table, linked back via that subsystem's component/paymentarea/itemid convention. Phase 4.3 decision: component = mod_confcheckin, paymentarea = 'tickettype', itemid = confcheckin_tickettype.id (the ticket type being bought, NOT a pre-created pending ticket row -- unlike some payable models, nothing is inserted into this table until classes/payment/service_provider.php::deliver_order() runs after a successful payment, matching enrol_fee's pattern of only calling enrol_user() in deliver_order() rather than pre-creating a pending enrolment).

FIELD.NAME: confcheckin
FIELD.COMMENT: Foreign key to confcheckin table

FIELD.NAME: tickettypeid
FIELD.COMMENT: Foreign key to confcheckin_tickettype table

FIELD.NAME: userid
FIELD.COMMENT: Foreign key to user table; the ticket holder

FIELD.NAME: origin
FIELD.COMMENT: One of purchase, free, promo or grant (Phase 4.5 follow-up: auto-granted via a linked group/enrolment method); enforced in PHP, not a DB CHECK constraint

FIELD.NAME: promocodeid
FIELD.COMMENT: References confcheckin_promocode.id when origin = promo, else null. Deliberately not declared as a DB-level foreign key, matching the exact key list this table was specified with

FIELD.NAME: qrtoken
FIELD.COMMENT: A unique, non-guessable random token identifying this ticket's QR code. MUST be generated from a cryptographically-random source (e.g. random_bytes()/random_string(), never a sequential or predictable value) when implemented in a later phase -- a guessable token would let anyone forge/scan another attendee's check-in

INDEX.NAME: qrtoken
INDEX.COMMENT: The lookup key the QR scanner will use

TABLE.NAME: confcheckin_checkin
TABLE.COMMENT: A recorded check-in event for an issued ticket

FIELD.NAME: ticketid
FIELD.COMMENT: Foreign key to confcheckin_ticket table

FIELD.NAME: scannedby
FIELD.COMMENT: Foreign key to user table; whoever performed the scan

FIELD.NAME: timecreated
FIELD.COMMENT: When the check-in was recorded

INDEX.NAME: ticketid
INDEX.COMMENT: One check-in per ticket; the schema itself makes a second check-in row for the same ticket impossible. Re-scanning an already-checked-in ticket is a later phase's business logic to reject gracefully client-side, but the DB constraint is the authoritative guarantee

TABLE.NAME: confcheckin_template
TABLE.COMMENT: Organiser-edited TinyMCE templates for badge/ticket/receipt/certificate PDFs

FIELD.NAME: confcheckin
FIELD.COMMENT: Foreign key to confcheckin table

FIELD.NAME: templatetype
FIELD.COMMENT: One of badge, ticket, receipt or certificate; enforced in PHP

FIELD.NAME: content
FIELD.COMMENT: TinyMCE-authored HTML with embeddable placeholder fields (e.g. {{fullname}}, {{tickettype}}); placeholder rendering itself is implemented in a later phase

FIELD.NAME: contentformat
FIELD.COMMENT: Standard Moodle text-format field for the content column, same type/length/default convention as this plugin's own introformat field (no existing rich-text field with its own format column exists yet in the sibling plugins' shipped schemas to match instead)

INDEX.NAME: confcheckintype
INDEX.COMMENT: At most one template per type per instance

TABLE.NAME: confcheckin_promocode
TABLE.COMMENT: Organiser-issued free/discount codes, redeemable for a specific ticket type

FIELD.NAME: confcheckin
FIELD.COMMENT: Foreign key to confcheckin table

FIELD.NAME: code
FIELD.COMMENT: The redeemable code string

FIELD.NAME: tickettypeid
FIELD.COMMENT: Foreign key to confcheckin_tickettype table; which ticket type this code grants for free

FIELD.NAME: maxuses
FIELD.COMMENT: Maximum number of redemptions; null means unlimited

FIELD.NAME: timeexpires
FIELD.COMMENT: Unix timestamp after which the code can no longer be redeemed; null means it never expires

INDEX.NAME: confcheckincode
INDEX.COMMENT: A code is unique within one instance, not necessarily globally
